narrative:
Dear Student,

I understand that receiving a rejection for your university application can be disheartening, and I want to help you understand the reasons behind this decision. The model used to evaluate your application predicted a 58.5% probability that you might not pass the bar exam on your first attempt. This prediction led to the rejection of your application, as the university aims to admit students who are likely to succeed in passing the bar exam.

Let's look at the factors that influenced this prediction. Your academic performance, particularly your grades in Year 1 and Year 3, were significant contributors. Your decile rankings in these years were lower than the average, which pushed the prediction towards a potential failure. Additionally, your LSAT score, which was below the average, also played a crucial role in this decision, as it is a strong indicator of your readiness for law school.

Your family income bracket, categorized as middle, and your racial background as Hispanic were also considered. While these factors had some influence, they were less significant compared to your academic performance and LSAT score. Your undergraduate GPA was slightly below average, which further contributed to the prediction of potential failure. On the other hand, being a full-time student and your gender had minimal impact on the decision.

Given this information, you might consider focusing on improving your academic performance and LSAT score if you plan to reapply in the future. These are areas where you have the potential to make changes that could positively influence your chances of passing the bar exam. Remember, this prediction is not a definitive measure of your abilities or potential, but rather a reflection of the data available at this time. With dedication and effort, you can work towards achieving your goal of passing the bar exam and gaining admission to the university.

I hope this explanation provides clarity and guidance as you plan your next steps. Please feel free to reach out if you have any further questions or need additional support.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
